title: Grieving families, law enforcement, Republican legislators tell Public Safety Committee that inaction will cause even more deaths.

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Fentanyl overdoses have accelerated in California since 2021, resulting in 500 Californians losing their lives to fentanyl each month.",
    "Inaction by the Assembly Public Safety Committee will lead to thousands of Californians being killed by fentanyl in the coming months.",
    "More than a hundred Californians lose their lives to fentanyl poisoning each week.",
    "The Assembly Public Safety Committee has killed a number of bills aimed at combating the fentanyl epidemic.",
    "AB 18 would warn dealers they could be convicted of murder if someone dies from drugs they sold.",
    "AB 701 would impose fines on fentanyl dealers for the damage they inflict on communities.",
    "AB 1508 would recognize the extremely potent nature of fentanyl and increase penalties for dealers.",
    "AB 33 aims to create a Fentanyl Addiction and Overdose Prevention Task Force.",
    "AB 955 seeks to increase penalties for selling fentanyl through social media.",
    "A dump truck capable of holding 28,000 pounds was displayed at the press conference, representing the weight of fentanyl seized in California last year."
  ]
}
```
